congo.explanation: Dikembe Mutombo was famous for blocking shots and wagging his finger — he was a 4-time NBA Defensive Player of the Year!

congo.question: What is special about the DRC's rainforest?
options: It's the smallest in the world
It's the second-largest in the world
congo.explanation: The DRC has the second-largest rainforest on Earth, after the Amazon — it's a treasure chest of biodiversity!

congo.question: What mineral mined in the DRC is used in smartphones and electronics?
congo.explanation: Coltan is a mineral used to make parts for smartphones, laptops, and game consoles — the DRC supplies much of the world's coltan!

congo.question: What is unique about the capital cities of the DRC and the Republic of Congo?
options: They are the two closest capital cities in the world
They are on the same side of the river
They were built by the same person
congo.explanation: Kinshasa (DRC) and Brazzaville (Republic of Congo) sit right across the Congo River from each other — the closest capital cities in the world!

algeria.question: What makes Algeria unique among African countries by size?
options: It's the largest country in Africa by area
algeria.explanation: Algeria is the largest country in Africa — it's about 3.5 times the size of Texas!

algeria.question: About how much of Algeria is covered by the Sahara Desert?
algeria.explanation: About 80% of Algeria is Sahara Desert — that's a LOT of sand! But the northern part has green mountains and a beautiful coast.

algeria.question: What do the ancient rock paintings at Tassili n'Ajjer tell us about the Sahara?
options: It was once green with rivers and animals
algeria.explanation: Rock paintings over 10,000 years old show giraffes, hippos, and rivers — proof that the Sahara was once a lush, green place!

algeria.question: What is Algeria's national animal?
algeria.explanation: The Fennec Fox is the world's smallest fox — its giant ears help it stay cool in the scorching Sahara heat!

algeria.question: What ancient civilization built the city of Timgad in Algeria?
algeria.explanation: The Romans built Timgad around 100 AD — you can still visit its remarkably well-preserved ruins today!

algeria.question: What is couscous?
options: Tiny steamed grain served with vegetables and meat
algeria.explanation: Couscous is a staple food in Algeria and across North Africa — it's tiny steamed grain often served with delicious stews!

zimbabwe.question: What does the name 'Zimbabwe' mean?
zimbabwe.explanation: Zimbabwe means 'House of Stone' in the Shona language — named after the Great Zimbabwe stone ruins!

zimbabwe.question: What is the local name for Victoria Falls?
zimbabwe.explanation: Victoria Falls is locally called 'Mosi-oa-Tunya,' meaning 'The Smoke That Thunders' — its mist can be seen from 30 miles away!

zimbabwe.question: What is the largest denomination banknote Zimbabwe ever printed?
zimbabwe.explanation: Zimbabwe printed a 100 TRILLION dollar note during hyperinflation — it's now a popular collector's item!

zimbabwe.question: What is the mbira?
zimbabwe.explanation: The mbira is a thumb piano made of metal keys on a wooden board — it's been played in Zimbabwe for over 1,000 years!

zimbabwe.question: What was Great Zimbabwe?
options: A massive stone city built without mortar between the 1100s and 1400s
zimbabwe.explanation: Great Zimbabwe was an amazing stone city built without any cement or mortar — the stones fit together perfectly through skilled engineering!

zimbabwe.question: Which Zimbabwean swimmer is the most decorated Olympic athlete in African history?
zimbabwe.explanation: Kirsty Coventry won 7 Olympic medals in swimming — more than any other African athlete in history!

senegal.question: What is Senegal's national sport?
senegal.explanation: Senegal's national sport is Laamb — a traditional wrestling style that includes mystical rituals and protective charms!

senegal.question: Why is Gorée Island historically important?
options: It was a vacation spot for kings
It was a major site in the transatlantic slave trade
It was where gold was discovered
It was the first place in Africa to have electricity
senegal.explanation: Gorée Island was a major departure point during the slave trade. Today it's a UNESCO World Heritage Site and a place of remembrance.

senegal.question: What does 'Teranga' mean in Senegalese culture?
senegal.explanation: Teranga means hospitality — Senegal is known as the 'Land of Teranga' because guests are treated like royalty!

senegal.question: How tall is the African Renaissance Monument in Dakar?
options: 160 feet — taller than the Statue of Liberty
senegal.explanation: The African Renaissance Monument stands 160 feet tall — even taller than the Statue of Liberty!

senegal.question: What amazing thing did Senegal do at the 2002 FIFA World Cup?
options: They beat France in the opening match in their first-ever World Cup
senegal.explanation: In their very first World Cup appearance, Senegal shocked the world by beating defending champions France and reaching the quarterfinals!

senegal.question: What is Thieboudienne?
options: Senegal's national dish of fish and rice in tomato sauce
senegal.explanation: Thieboudienne is Senegal's beloved national dish — flavorful fish and rice cooked in a rich tomato sauce. Delicious!

rwanda.question: Why is Rwanda called the 'Land of a Thousand Hills'?
options: Its beautiful landscape is covered in rolling green hills
rwanda.explanation: Rwanda's gorgeous landscape is filled with rolling green hills everywhere you look — earning it this beautiful nickname!

rwanda.question: What world record does Rwanda hold about its parliament?
options: Highest percentage of women in parliament (over 60%)
rwanda.explanation: Rwanda has more women in parliament than any other country in the world — over 60%! That's an incredible achievement.

rwanda.question: What is Umuganda?
options: A monthly national community service day
rwanda.explanation: On the last Saturday of every month, all Rwandans participate in Umuganda — a national cleanup and community service day!

rwanda.question: What endangered animal can you see in Rwanda's Volcanoes National Park?
rwanda.explanation: Fewer than 1,000 mountain gorillas remain in the wild, and many live in Rwanda's Volcanoes National Park!

rwanda.question: What innovative technology did Rwanda pioneer for delivering medical supplies?
rwanda.explanation: Rwanda was one of the first countries to use drones to deliver blood and medical supplies to remote hospitals — saving many lives!

rwanda.question: What item is completely banned in Rwanda, making it one of the cleanest countries?
rwanda.explanation: Rwanda banned single-use plastic bags — if you try to bring one in, it'll be taken at the border! This helped make Rwanda super clean.

madagascar.question: What percentage of Madagascar's wildlife is found NOWHERE else on Earth?
madagascar.explanation: About 90% of Madagascar's animals and plants are endemic — meaning they exist only in Madagascar! The island split from Africa over 160 million years ago.

madagascar.question: What product does Madagascar supply about 80% of to the world?
madagascar.explanation: Madagascar produces about 80% of the world's vanilla — so your vanilla ice cream and cookies probably have Malagasy vanilla in them!

madagascar.question: What is the fossa?
options: A cat-like predator and the largest carnivore on Madagascar
madagascar.explanation: The fossa looks like a mix between a cat and a mongoose — it's the top predator on Madagascar and found nowhere else!

madagascar.question: How did the first people reach Madagascar?
options: They sailed from Southeast Asia over 3,500 miles away
madagascar.explanation: Amazingly, the first settlers came from Southeast Asia — over 3,500 miles across the Indian Ocean — about 2,000 years ago!

madagascar.question: What makes the Avenue of the Baobabs special?
options: It has the tallest trees in the world
It features ancient trees over 800 years old that look upside down
It's the longest road in Africa
madagascar.explanation: The Avenue of the Baobabs features massive trees over 800 years old — they look like they've been planted upside down with roots sticking up!

madagascar.question: Where does Madagascar rank among the world's largest islands?
madagascar.explanation: Madagascar is the 4th largest island in the world — after Greenland, New Guinea, and Borneo!

nepal.question: What is the tallest mountain on Earth, located in Nepal?
nepal.explanation: Mount Everest stands at 29,032 feet — that's almost as high as a jet plane flies! Nepalis call it 'Sagarmatha,' meaning 'Goddess of the Sky.'

nepal.question: What makes Nepal's flag unique among all countries?
options: It's the smallest flag in the world
nepal.explanation: Nepal is the ONLY country in the world with a non-rectangular flag! It's made of two stacked triangles in crimson red with blue borders.

nepal.question: Who was born in Lumbini, Nepal around 563 BC?
nepal.explanation: Siddhartha Gautama, who became the Buddha and founded Buddhism, was born in Lumbini, Nepal. It's now a UNESCO World Heritage Site!

nepal.question: What is the most popular street food in Nepal?
nepal.explanation: Momos are steamed dumplings filled with meat or vegetables. They're Nepal's favorite snack and you can find them on almost every street corner!

nepal.question: How many of the world's ten tallest mountains are in Nepal?
nepal.explanation: Eight of the world's ten tallest mountains are in Nepal! That's why Nepal is called the 'Roof of the World.'

nepal.question: What animal is it illegal to harm in Nepal because it's the national animal?
nepal.explanation: The cow is Nepal's national animal and is considered sacred. It's illegal to slaughter cows in Nepal!

mongolia.question: Who founded the largest land empire in history?
mongolia.explanation: Genghis Khan united the Mongolian tribes in 1206 and built an empire stretching from China to Europe — the largest connected land empire ever!

mongolia.question: What is Mongolia famous for in terms of population density?
mongolia.explanation: Mongolia is the most sparsely populated country in the world — only about 2 people per square kilometer. That's a LOT of open space!

mongolia.question: What are the 'Three Manly Games' celebrated at Mongolia's Naadam Festival?
mongolia.explanation: Naadam features wrestling, horse racing, and archery — sports Mongolians have practiced for thousands of years. Kids as young as 5 ride in the horse races!

mongolia.question: What unique hunting tradition uses trained golden eagles?
mongolia.explanation: Kazakh eagle hunters in western Mongolia train golden eagles to hunt foxes and wolves. This tradition is over 4,000 years old!

mongolia.question: What does Mongolia have more of than people?
mongolia.explanation: Mongolia has about 4 million horses but only 3.4 million people! Mongolian kids often learn to ride horses before they can walk.

mongolia.question: What is a ger (yurt)?
options: A portable felt tent used by nomads
mongolia.explanation: A ger (yurt) is a portable round tent covered in felt. About 30% of Mongolians still live in gers, and they can be set up or taken down in just a few hours!

panama.question: What famous waterway connects the Atlantic and Pacific Oceans through Panama?
panama.explanation: The Panama Canal is a 50-mile waterway that saves ships a 12,000-mile trip around South America. About 14,000 ships pass through it every year!

panama.question: What is unique about sunrises and sunsets in Panama?
options: You can see the sun rise over the Pacific and set over the Atlantic
There are two sunsets per day
The sun rises in the west
panama.explanation: Because Panama curves in an S-shape, you can watch the sun rise over the Pacific Ocean and set over the Atlantic — the opposite of what you'd expect!

panama.question: Who is the greatest baseball closer of all time, from Panama?
panama.explanation: Mariano Rivera from Panama is the only player in MLB history to be unanimously voted into the Baseball Hall of Fame. He's the all-time saves leader!

panama.question: What is Panama's national bird?
panama.explanation: The Harpy Eagle is one of the largest and most powerful eagles in the world. It can carry prey equal to its own body weight!

panama.question: How many years did it take to build the Panama Canal?
panama.explanation: The Panama Canal took 10 years to build (1904-1914) and required over 75,000 workers. It's considered one of the greatest engineering feats in history!

panama.question: What tiny bright yellow animal is a national symbol of Panama?
panama.explanation: The Panamanian Golden Frog is a tiny, bright yellow frog that is considered a symbol of good luck. Sadly, it's critically endangered in the wild.

botswana.question: What is the world's largest inland river delta, located in Botswana?
botswana.explanation: The Okavango Delta is the world's largest inland delta — instead of flowing into the ocean, the river flows into the Kalahari Desert, creating a paradise for wildlife!

botswana.question: What precious stone transformed Botswana from one of the poorest to one of the wealthiest countries in Africa?
botswana.explanation: Diamonds were discovered in Botswana in 1967, just one year after independence. The country used diamond wealth wisely to build schools, hospitals, and roads!

botswana.question: What does Botswana's currency name 'Pula' mean?
botswana.explanation: 'Pula' means 'rain' in Setswana. In a dry country like Botswana, rain is so precious and valued that they named their money after it!

botswana.question: Which country has the largest population of African elephants?
botswana.explanation: Botswana has over 130,000 African elephants — more than any other country! Chobe National Park is especially famous for its huge elephant herds.

botswana.question: What desert covers about 70% of Botswana?
botswana.explanation: The Kalahari Desert covers most of Botswana. It's home to the San people, one of the oldest cultures on Earth, who have lived there for tens of thousands of years!

botswana.question: What standing animal is Botswana's national animal, seen on the country's coat of arms?
botswana.explanation: The zebra is Botswana's national animal! The black and white stripes on the flag were inspired by the zebra, symbolizing harmony between people.

jordan.question: What ancient city carved into rose-red cliffs is one of the New Seven Wonders of the World?
jordan.explanation: Petra was built by the Nabataeans over 2,000 years ago. The buildings were carved directly into the cliff faces — no stacking stones needed!

jordan.question: What body of water in Jordan is the lowest point on Earth?
jordan.explanation: The Dead Sea is 1,412 feet below sea level — the lowest point on Earth! It's so salty (almost 10 times saltier than the ocean) that you float without trying.

jordan.question: What famous movie featured Petra's Treasury building?
options: Indiana Jones and the Last Crusade
jordan.explanation: Indiana Jones rides up to Petra's Treasury in 'The Last Crusade.' The desert valley of Wadi Rum was also used as Mars in 'The Martian'!

jordan.question: Why is the Dead Sea called 'dead'?
options: It's too salty for fish and most life to survive
jordan.explanation: The Dead Sea is so extremely salty (about 34% salt) that almost no fish, plants, or other organisms can survive in it. That's why it's called 'dead'!

jordan.question: What sport earned Jordan its first-ever Olympic gold medal in 2016?
jordan.explanation: Ahmad Abughaush won Jordan's first-ever Olympic medal — and it was gold! He won in taekwondo at the 2016 Rio Olympics.

jordan.question: What is Jordan's national dish?
jordan.explanation: Mansaf is lamb cooked in a special dried yogurt sauce and served over rice. It's traditionally eaten with your right hand at big celebrations!

belgium.question: What musical instrument was invented by Belgian Adolphe Sax?
belgium.explanation: The saxophone was invented by Belgian Adolphe Sax in the 1840s. Can you imagine jazz, rock, or pop music without the sax?

belgium.question: What popular food was actually invented in Belgium, not the country in its name?
belgium.explanation: French fries are actually Belgian! Belgians have been frying potatoes since the 1600s. The name 'French' probably came from American soldiers in World War I who spoke French in Belgium.

belgium.question: What famous comic book character was created by Belgian artist Hergé?
belgium.explanation: Tintin is one of the most popular comic book characters in the world! Belgium is also the home of the Smurfs and Lucky Luke.

belgium.question: What major international organization has its headquarters in Brussels, Belgium?
belgium.explanation: Brussels is the unofficial capital of the European Union. Many EU institutions are headquartered there, making it one of the most important political cities in the world.

belgium.question: What industry has made Antwerp, Belgium famous for over 500 years?
belgium.explanation: Antwerp has been the diamond capital of the world for over 500 years! About 80% of the world's rough diamonds pass through the city.

belgium.question: How many official languages does Belgium have?
belgium.explanation: Belgium has three official languages: Dutch, French, and German! The country has separate regions for each language community.

trinidadandtobago.question: What musical instrument, made from oil barrels, was invented in Trinidad?
trinidadandtobago.explanation: The steelpan is the only acoustic musical instrument invented in the 20th century! People in Trinidad turned discarded oil barrels into beautiful instruments in the 1930s.

trinidadandtobago.question: What famous dance where you bend backward under a bar originated in Trinidad?
trinidadandtobago.explanation: The limbo dance started in Trinidad! The tradition was inspired by funeral ceremonies where dancers moved under a bar to symbolize the passage to the afterlife.

trinidadandtobago.question: What cricket record does Trinidadian Brian Lara hold?
options: Highest individual Test score (400 not out)
trinidadandtobago.explanation: Brian Lara scored 400 not out in a single Test innings in 2004 — the highest individual score in Test cricket history! He's a national hero in Trinidad.

trinidadandtobago.question: What is the most popular street food in Trinidad and Tobago?
trinidadandtobago.explanation: Doubles are two soft flatbreads filled with curried chickpeas and topped with various sauces. They're cheap, delicious, and eaten at any time of day!

trinidadandtobago.question: What natural wonder in Trinidad is the largest of its kind in the world?
trinidadandtobago.explanation: Pitch Lake is the largest natural asphalt lake in the world, covering about 100 acres. Its asphalt has been used to pave roads around the world, even in New York City!

trinidadandtobago.question: What item of clothing is it illegal to wear in Trinidad?
trinidadandtobago.explanation: It's illegal for civilians to wear camouflage clothing in Trinidad and Tobago — it's reserved exclusively for military personnel. You could even get arrested for wearing it!
